facts.text: Manhasset’s name comes from a Native American word meaning “the island neighborhood.”
The hamlet sits on the Harbor Hill Moraine, a terminal moraine formed by glaciers during the Wisconsin Glacial Episode.
In 1898 the Long Island Railroad reached Manhasset, bringing wealthy New Yorkers seeking country homes with easy access to Manhattan.
The Americana Manhasset shopping center began in 1956 when Gerace & Catagna Inc., a masonry firm founded in 1922, purchased the land.
The Manhasset Valley School, originally built for Gold Coast estate children, later served the African American community before closing in the 1960s after a desegregation lawsuit.
Manhasset hosted the 2007‑2008 negotiations between Morocco and the Polisario Front over the Western Sahara conflict.
